codeReview.summary: The data integrity feature is comprehensively implemented. All four validation assertions (VAL-REL-001 through VAL-REL-004) are fully addressed: FK constraint on spikes.share_id, removal of denormalized tier column (now via JOIN), removal of spike_count column (now via COUNT(*)), and server-side UUID generation. The migration script is well-structured with proper transaction handling and the code changes follow existing patterns in the modularized worker architecture.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature follows documented patterns in AGENTS.md for TypeScript (strict mode, Zod validation) and matches the modularized worker structure (handlers, db/queries, utils separation).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/db/queries.ts uses proper typed queries with Env interface; worker/src/handlers/spikes.ts uses Zod schema validation; worker/src/types.ts has proper TypeScript interfaces

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the SKILL.md procedure: read feature description, understood current code, wrote tests first (TDD pattern visible in commit), created migration file, implemented feature, ran all tests (50 pass), ran typecheck, and committed with descriptive message.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff file shows 9 new test cases added in data-integrity.test.ts before implementation; migration 003_data_integrity.sql created; npx vitest run shows 50 tests passing; npx tsc --noEmit passes

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands needed - existing test-worker and typecheck-worker commands in services.yaml cover validation needs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml already has test-worker and typecheck-worker commands which the worker used for verification

sharedStateObservations.observation: The FK constraint test uses a pragmatic approach - it documents that D1 FK constraints may vary in enforcement and includes error message checking. This is useful knowledge for future developers working with D1.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test/data-integrity.test.ts:197-223 - the test includes a comment about FK constraint enforcement and checks for multiple possible error message patterns

summary: Data integrity feature passes review. All schema changes properly implemented via migration, denormalized columns removed and replaced with proper queries (JOIN for tier, COUNT(*) for counts), server-side UUID generation enforced, and comprehensive test coverage added. No blocking issues found.
